title: Manage The Day (MTD) – AI Productivity Platform
summary: A cross-platform AI productivity platform — a unified multi-provider calendar, a voice + text AI planning assistant, and live iOS Dynamic Island timers.
overview: MTD is a production-grade productivity platform I lead as the Flutter engineer at Connecting Points Tech. A single Flutter codebase ships to iOS, Android, and Web; it unifies four external calendar providers, runs a voice + text AI planning assistant, and drives live iOS Dynamic Island timers — bringing a person's entire day (tasks, projects, goals, and time) into one place.
details: **The problem** — People juggle their day across disconnected tools: tasks in one app; Google, Apple, Outlook and the phone's own calendar in others; goals and time-tracking nowhere at all. Planning means manually reconciling all of it, every day.

**Unified multi-provider calendar** — The hardest and most distinctive piece. I built a single calendar surface (on Syncfusion) that merges MTD's own events with **Google Calendar, Apple/iCloud via CalDAV, Microsoft 365 via Graph, and on-device iOS via EventKit** — each with different auth and sync semantics — into one colour-coded view supporting drag-and-drop, resizing, recurrence, and timezone-correct scheduling.

**Live iOS activities** — Integrated **ActivityKit / Live Activities** so an active focus timer shows live on the Dynamic Island and lock screen, mirrored with a native Android background service — keeping the current task visible without opening the app.

**Voice + text AI assistant** — An in-app assistant (FastAPI + **Google Agent Development Kit**, Genkit, and Firebase Functions) that takes natural language — by text or voice — and manages tasks, events, goals, and pillars, and **auto-schedules** work onto the calendar via tool-calling.

**Full productivity system** — Task Cloud (kanban with subtasks and drag-to-schedule), Pillars and Goals for life-area planning, Projects, Focus Time / Time Boxing, Time Entries, and a Reporting view with per-pillar analytics (tracked vs target, efficiency, active days).

**Architecture** — A single Flutter (Dart 3) codebase with layered state management: **BLoC** for long-lived global state, **Provider** for UI-reactive state (timers, network status), and **Riverpod** for MCP tokens and audit logs. Firebase handles Auth, Firestore (real-time sync), Storage, and Analytics; a **FastAPI** backend is reached over REST via Dio.

**My role & impact** — I own the Flutter architecture and the platform-specific hard problems — cross-provider calendar sync, Live Activities, real-time data flow, and the assistant's client integration — taking features from design to a shipped, production cross-platform product.

title: Crooze – Fitness Challenge App
summary: A community fitness-challenge app — cycling and walking contests, leaderboards, and automated certificate generation.
overview: Crooze is a community-driven fitness app (iOS & Android) where users join admin-hosted cycling and walking challenges to earn rewards and certificates. As a software engineer at The Internet Folks I shipped three challenge modules (Community, Club, Individual) plus leaderboards and automated certificates, then hardened a live product — cutting GraphQL over-fetching and resolving crash loops and Android Binder transaction failures.
details: **Features I shipped** — Community, Club, and Individual challenge modules, a Leaderboard system, challenge-summary views, and automated certificate generation for challenge winners.
**Performance via GraphQL** — Integrated GraphQL APIs and tuned queries to cut over-fetching across challenge and profile screens, reducing payloads and latency.

**Stability engineering** — Diagnosed and fixed crash loops, null-reference crashes, and state inconsistencies; a notable fix was resolving **Android Binder transaction failures** by throttling isolate updates and optimising background data processing.
**My role** — Feature development and reliability work on a shipped consumer app, with BLoC state management on Firebase.

title: AgreEasy – B2B Agriculture Platform
summary: A multilingual B2B agriculture platform where admins send crop-specific sowing requests that farmers accept or reject — plus land allocation & inspection, price negotiation, irrigation, tasks, and in-app support.
overview: AgreEasy is a multilingual B2B agriculture platform (iOS & Android) connecting farmers with buyers. Its core loop: an admin raises demand for specific crops and sends sowing requests to farmers, who accept or reject them. Around that sit land allocation and inspection, price negotiation, irrigation and task management, and in-app support with training. As a software engineer at The Internet Folks I built core farmer-facing modules end to end and hardened a live product.
details: **Sowing requests (the core loop)** — admins raise demand for specific crops and send sowing requests to farmers, who review the crop, quantity, offered price and sowing window and **accept or reject** each request.

**Farm & land management** — farmer onboarding (auth, profile), an Add-Farm flow with soil, irrigation and government-ID fields (Tehsil/Block) plus validation, land allocation, and land-inspection workflows.
**Trade & operations** — price negotiation between farmers and buyers, irrigation facilities, and task tracking.
**Support & training** — in-app ticket raising with live chat for any issue, and embedded video demos to properly train farmers.
**Multilingual** — full localization (e.g., Hindi) so regional farmers use the app in their own language.

**Reliability & my role** — as a software engineer at The Internet Folks (iOS & Android) I optimised the ticketing system, fixed notification-delivery bugs, and eliminated memory leaks, with Riverpod state management on a GraphQL + Firebase stack.

title: SimbaCourse – Education Management App
summary: A full-stack school platform replacing pen-and-paper — online attendance, real-time tracking, enrollment, fees & dues, online exams, and course management, on mobile and desktop.
overview: SimbaCourse is an education-management platform that moves schools from pen-and-paper to software. Teachers take attendance online, students track their attendance in real time, and admins handle enrollment, fees, exams and courses — from one app that runs on both mobile and desktop. Over a six-month SDE internship at SimbaQuartz I built it full-stack: a Flutter (Provider) client, an Express.js + MongoDB backend, Firebase Auth, and AWS S3 for media.
details: **From paper to software** — replaced manual pen-and-paper workflows: teachers take attendance online and students track their attendance in real time.
**Student lifecycle** — student enrollment, fee payment, a dues tracker, and student–teacher relationship mapping.
**Academics** — online exams, course details, and domain & training management for the institution.
**Full-stack build** — a Flutter client (Provider state management) for both mobile and desktop; an Express.js + MongoDB backend; Firebase Auth for sign-in; and AWS S3 for file & media storage.
**My role** — end-to-end delivery over a six-month internship: data model, REST API, and the shipped cross-platform app.
